Legal Document

Terms & Conditions

Last updated: June 2026  |  Please read carefully before using NBF Connect

Table of Contents

  1. Acceptance of Terms
  2. Eligibility
  3. User Accounts
  4. Shurveer (Candidate) Responsibilities
  5. Employer Responsibilities
  6. Platform Limitations & Disclaimer
  7. Prohibited Activities
  8. Intellectual Property
  9. Account Termination
  10. Governing Law
  11. Contact

1 Acceptance of Terms

By accessing or using NBF Connect ("Platform"), operated by National Bharat Force, you agree to be bound by these Terms & Conditions. These terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and National Bharat Force.

If you do not agree to these terms, you must not use this platform. We reserve the right to update these terms at any time, with notice provided via email and platform notification.

2 Eligibility

To register as a Shurveer on NBF Connect, you must:

  • Be a current or former member of at least one of: NCC, NSS, Scouts & Guides, NYKS, or Civil Defence
  • Be aged 15 years or above (users aged 15–18 require parental consent)
  • Be a citizen or resident of India
  • Provide accurate and truthful information during registration
  • Have a valid Indian mobile number and email address

To register as an Employer, you must:

  • Represent a legitimate registered organisation, company, or institution in India
  • Have authority to post opportunities on behalf of your organisation
  • Comply with all applicable Indian labour laws
Important: Providing false information about your organisation membership will result in immediate account termination and may be reported to the relevant organisation authorities.

3 User Accounts

  • You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials
  • You must not share your account with any other person
  • You must notify us immediately at support@nbfconnect.in if you suspect unauthorised access to your account
  • One person may maintain only one Shurveer account
  • You are responsible for all activities that occur under your account

4 Shurveer Responsibilities

As a registered Shurveer, you agree to:

  • Provide accurate, complete, and up-to-date profile information
  • Upload genuine certificates from your NCC/NSS/Scouts/NYKS/Civil Defence organisation
  • Not misrepresent your skills, qualifications, or experience
  • Respond professionally to employer communications
  • Uphold the values of discipline, integrity, and service as stated in your registration oath
  • Not use the platform for any purpose other than legitimate employment/internship seeking
  • Keep your profile information updated

5 Employer Responsibilities

As a registered Employer, you agree to:

  • Post only genuine, legal, and available opportunities
  • Not post opportunities that violate Indian labour laws or are discriminatory
  • Respond to applicants in a timely and professional manner
  • Not collect any fees from Shurveers for applying or placing them in roles
  • Not use candidate data for any purpose other than the specific recruitment process
  • Remove opportunities once they are filled or no longer available
  • Verify your organisation's legitimacy if requested by NBF Connect
Zero Tolerance: Any employer found charging fees from candidates, posting fake jobs, or misusing candidate data will be permanently banned and reported to appropriate authorities.

6 Platform Limitations & Disclaimer

NBF Connect is a connecting platform only. We do not:

  • Guarantee employment or placement for any Shurveer
  • Verify every opportunity posted by employers beyond basic checks
  • Take responsibility for the outcome of any employment relationship
  • Guarantee the accuracy of employer-provided information
  • Act as an employment agency or recruiter

The platform is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We are not liable for any direct, indirect, or consequential losses arising from the use of this platform.

7 Prohibited Activities

The following are strictly prohibited on NBF Connect:

  • Creating fake profiles or impersonating any person or organisation
  • Uploading forged or manipulated certificates
  • Spamming other users with unsolicited messages
  • Attempting to hack, scrape, or reverse-engineer the platform
  • Posting offensive, discriminatory, or illegal content
  • Using the platform for multi-level marketing or pyramid schemes
  • Creating multiple accounts to circumvent bans or restrictions
  • Any activity that violates Indian law including the IT Act 2000

8 Intellectual Property

All content on NBF Connect including the logo, name, design, code, and platform features are the intellectual property of National Bharat Force. You may not copy, reproduce, or use any part of this platform without written permission.

By uploading your resume, photo, or other content, you grant NBF Connect a non-exclusive licence to display and use this content for the purpose of operating the platform.

9 Account Termination

NBF Connect reserves the right to suspend or permanently terminate any account that:

  • Violates these Terms & Conditions
  • Provides false information during registration or verification
  • Engages in prohibited activities listed in Section 7
  • Receives multiple verified complaints from other users
  • Remains inactive for more than 24 months

Users may request account deletion at any time by emailing support@nbfconnect.in. Upon deletion, all personal data will be removed within 30 days except where retention is required by law.

10 Governing Law

These Terms & Conditions are governed by the laws of India. Any disputes arising from the use of NBF Connect shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of courts in Hyderabad, Telangana, India.

For disputes, users are encouraged to first contact our Grievance Officer before initiating legal proceedings.

11 Contact

For Terms-Related Queries

Founder, NBF Connect | National Bharat Force

Dilsukhnagar, Hyderabad, Telangana 500035

Email: support@nbfconnect.in

Response time: Within 30 days

NBF Connect

"Connecting disciplined youth with real opportunities and building a stronger, self-reliant Bharat."

Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports
